# UC-17.1.36 · Cisco TrustSec / SGT Assignment Mismatch and SXP Listener Health

> **Criticality:** High &middot; **Difficulty:** Advanced &middot; **Pillar:** Security &middot; **Type:** Configuration, Compliance, Operations &middot; **Wave:** Walk &middot; **Status:** Verified

*When ISE tells switches and routers which group each user belongs to, those switches use that group label to allow or deny traffic. We watch the live conversation between ISE and the network gear — if the conversation drops, the group labels stop being delivered and security rules silently stop working. We catch that.*

---

## Description

Detects SXP peer flapping (≥2 downs in 15 min), SXP binding-update failures, and SGT assignment mismatches between ISE and the NAD. SGT-based segmentation only works when SGT mappings are consistent across ISE and the NADs that enforce — drift in either direction means a host either gets the wrong privileges or no privileges.

## Value

TrustSec/SGT segmentation is the core of zero-trust microsegmentation in Cisco environments. When a NAD's SXP peer to ISE flaps, *no SGT-based ACEs are enforced* on that NAD until the peer is re-established and the binding table is re-populated. Catching the flap quickly preserves segmentation enforcement.

## Implementation

Confirm the *TrustSec* logging category is enabled at INFO on ISE (UC-17.1.28 plumbing). Onboard NAD logs (IOS-XE/NX-OS/SD-WAN edge devices) into `index=network`. Schedule the search every 5 min, search window `-15m@m to @m`, trigger on count > 0, throttle suppression on `peer` for 30 min.

## Control test

### Positive scenario

On a lab NAD, configure an SXP peer to ISE then disable the peer config (`no cts sxp connection peer ...`). Within 60s ISE emits 83004 (SXP peer disconnected) and the NAD emits `%CTS-3-SXP_PEER_DOWN`. UC-17.1.36 fires showing the peer with `peer_downs >= 2` after a second flap.

### Negative scenario

Restore the SXP peer config. Within 60s the peer reconnects (83003 / `%CTS-6-SXP_PEER_UP`). UC-17.1.36 does NOT fire on the next schedule.

## Detailed Implementation

### Prerequisites
- Cisco TrustSec deployed: ISE has SGTs defined, NADs configured with `cts sxp` and `cts role-based enforcement`.
- `sxp_peers.csv` lookup with `peer_ip`, `peer_name`, `is_critical`, `site`.
- Splunk role with `srchIndexesAllowed = nac, network, radius`.

### Step 1 — Configure data collection
(a) ISE side — *TrustSec* category at INFO, forwarded.
(b) NAD side — IOS-XE/NX-OS syslog forwarded to Splunk, parsed via the appropriate Cisco network TA.

Verify both:
```spl
index=nac sourcetype="cisco:ise:syslog" MessageCode IN (83001, 83003, 83004, 83006) earliest=-7d | stats count by MessageCode
index=network sourcetype="cisco:ios" "CTS-" earliest=-7d | stats count by host
```

### Step 2 — Create the search
Search is in `spl` field. Schedule every 5 min, time range `-15m@m to @m`, throttle `peer` for 30 min.

### Step 3 — Validate
Provoke as in `controlTest`. Confirm UC fires.

### Step 4 — Operationalize
Dashboard "ISE — TrustSec / SXP Health":
- Single value: SXP peers up vs total.
- Timeline: peer events last 24h.
- Top-N: peers with most flaps last 7d.
- SGT distribution panel — sessions per SGT.

Alerting: PagerDuty medium on flap; high if multiple peers flap simultaneously (suggests ISE-side issue rather than peer-side).

Runbook:
1. Identify peer.
2. SSH to NAD: `show cts sxp connections`. Confirm peer state.
3. SSH to ISE PSN: `show cts sxp connection`. Confirm peer state from ISE side.
4. Validate keepalive timers match.
5. Validate IP reachability and TCP/64999 (default SXP port) on the path.

### Step 5 — Troubleshooting

- **Peers flapping every 90s** — keepalive mismatch. Standardise on the recommended hold-time min/max.

- **Binding updates failing on one peer** — peer's binding table is full; check `show cts sxp binding` on the peer.

- **NAD reports CTS-3 errors but ISE shows the peer up** — Asymmetric SXP path; the NAD's local view differs from ISE's. Manual `clear cts sxp connection peer` on the NAD usually resolves.

## SPL

```spl
(index=nac sourcetype="cisco:ise:syslog" MessageCode IN (83003, 83004, 83006))
  OR (index=network sourcetype="cisco:ios" ("CTS-3-SXP_PEER_DOWN" OR "CTS-3-SXP_BINDING_FAIL"))
| eval peer = coalesce(SXPPeerIP, src_ip, host)
| stats count latest(_time) as last_event, count(eval(MessageCode=="83004" OR like(_raw, "%CTS-3-SXP_PEER_DOWN%"))) as peer_downs, count(eval(MessageCode=="83006" OR like(_raw, "%CTS-3-SXP_BINDING_FAIL%"))) as binding_failures by peer
| where peer_downs >= 2 OR binding_failures >= 1
| sort - binding_failures - peer_downs
```

## Visualization

(1) Timeline: SXP peer up/down events last 24h, Y = peer, color by event class. (2) Top-N table: peers with most flaps last 7d. (3) SGT distribution panel: count of active sessions per SGT (from RADIUS accounting), last 1h — useful for capacity verification.

## Known False Positives

- **NAD reload during scheduled change** — A switch reload generates one SXP peer-down then peer-up. Cross-reference change tickets and suppress within the change window.
- **SXP keepalive misconfiguration** — Different keepalive timers between ISE and NAD cause repeated benign peer-flaps every 60–120s. Symptoms: peer_downs > 5 in an hour but no actual binding loss. Fix the keepalive config (`cts sxp connection peer ... password default mode local listener hold-time min 90 max 180`) and the alert noise resolves.
- **Brief WAN brown-out for remote-site NAD** — A brief WAN drop will flap the SXP peer but not necessarily lose binding state if the NAD has a short-term cache. Single isolated peer-down events should not page; threshold ≥2 in 15 min.

## References

- [Cisco TrustSec — Configuration Guide](https://www.cisco.com/c/en/us/td/docs/switches/lan/trustsec/configuration/guide/trustsec.html)
- [Cisco ISE 3.4 Admin Guide — TrustSec](https://www.cisco.com/c/en/us/td/docs/security/ise/3-4/admin_guide/b_ise_admin_3_4/m_segmentation.html)
- [Cisco SXP (Security Group Exchange Protocol) RFC-style spec](https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/draft-smith-kandula-sxp/)
